Anybody who will march in here and tell you about a satisfactory experience with him, so satisfactory that they are dying to be in business with him again. He's been around for 22 years. There's got to be one, right? This thing involves millions of dollars. Pay him $100,000 to produce one. He can't. I know he can't. His mother won't even come in here and swear for him. So I said, what makes you guys think you're going to be the first people in 22 years on the planet he doesn't screw and crap on? Why? Because you're like, blessed? I mean, I finally won the argument. So that's like an ironclad test. This deal about getting out of things. I wake up three mornings in a row thinking about you. We're not having sex. You got to go. Three in a row. You're gone. I don't care what it costs. I don't care how ugly it is because it's too big of a distraction. If you're interfering with my sleep, imagine what you're doing interfering to my waking hours. So you've got to go. So you've got to have litmus tests. You've got to have extreme sensitivity to time. You've got to have ruthless intolerance for incompetence. You can't keep people around you who just can't or won't do it. You can't. Brother in line. Look. If you have a relative who can't support himself or herself without you. First of all, there's a reason that is not going to be cured by you providing them with a job or creating a business for them. Especially if they're over the age of 40. Again, odds are sometime in those 40 years they have bumble their way into something that should have been able to support them. So it's not like lack of opportunity. There's some other thing at work here. Just support them. It's cheaper. I got one. Bought the house, paid for everything. I've flirted briefly one day. Maybe I should just create a job for this person since I'm going to pay for them anyway. Took me two hours. You know better. You knew better. Get out of this. About five times I have this temptation. Five different times down in five years I've had this temptation. Well, oh, that's a really good simple brain dead. You can't fuck this up business opportunity. Right. I could I could put this person in that. Right. Now, fortunately, I'm able to talk myself out of these things. If you are not able to talk yourself out of these things, find somebody in your life who will talk yourself who will talk you out of them for you. When I'm not able to talk myself out of them, generally Lee Miltier talks me out of them. So I call her and I say, I got this idea. Here's what I'm going to do for this person. And then she says, no, because here's what's going to happen. And she's right. Of course, I already knew it, but I wasn't good enough to talk myself out of it. So find somebody who's sold job. Now, don't spend a lot of time with them. When you need them, but you find the most negative, you know, and get tell them your job is when I think about hiring my brother in law, creating a business for my brother in law, doing anything for my brother in law, but bailing them out of jail and giving them money. You talk me out of it. That's your job. And put them on retainer if you want to. I mean, it'll be cheaper than them. But so how many of you have made the mistake? How many of you have created a position or a business, raise them nice and proud to come out? It's a badge of compassion. There you go. And it's as futile as the day is long. All it does, you know, the thing about you shouldn't try and teach a pig to sing because all it does is piss off the pig and you get covered with mud and slop. Right. Well, that's fundamentally it. That's where it ends up. And it's very hard to fire them, by the way. I mean, I fired my mother so twice. It's not and it's not easy to do, you know, it screws up Thanksgiving. It's it's hard on your dad. It's, you know, not good. So better not to be in that position at all in the first place. You can see.
